title: mentha × verticillata f. rubrohirta
intro: Mentha × verticillata f. rubrohirta, also known as Red-stemmed Wild Mint, is a subspecies of mint that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its red-stemmed and hairy leaves. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Mentha × verticillata f. rubrohirta include its ability to alleviate nausea, reduce fever, and promote respiratory health. The herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or applied topically as essential oils or salves to harness its medicinal properties.
